entry.title: Excavation Initiative
entry.content: Excavation Initiative Excavation Initiative rewards you for doing what you'd already be doing: playing Overwatch. Throughout the season, you'll earn Loot Boxes simply by logging in, completing challenges, winning games, and staying active week after week. The more you play, the more opportunities you'll have to uncover Base, Epic, and Legendary Loot Boxes packed with rewards. No complicated routes, no hidden catches… just a season-long treasure hunt with Venture leading the way. Additional Detai

entry.title: Less than a month before launch, survival RPG Outward 2 delayed into 2027
entry.content: Survival RPG Outward 2 has been delayed from a July 2026 release into next year. Developer Nine Dots Studio delivered the news on Steam today, with Nine Dots CEO and Outward 2 creative director Guillaume Boucher-Vidal saying "I apologize that we couldn’t give you the game you guys deserve on time.".. Read more.

entry.title: Anima Strike Meta Event
entry.content: Anima Strike Meta Event Anima Strike is a three-week event that unfolds as you fight your way through the new Tokyo map, where Shion reigns supreme. Conquer daily and weekly challenges to move across the city, discovering Hashimoto, Yokai, and Subway locations, all littered with lore. The deeper you push into Tokyo, the more you uncover, including choices to make on branching paths and fresh rewards to stack each week. Anima Strike is a narrative driven by momentum, told at your pace, your way.

entry.content: Are you ready to step Into the Tiger’s Den? A new chapter in the Reign of Talon starts here with the arrival of our newest Damage Hero, Shion. As the Hashimoto Clan’s most terrifying elder, Shion brings a dangerous new arsenal to the battlefield with her dual semi-auto pistols and a motorcycle she can crash into her enemies. entry.title: An obscure new game-breaking Destiny bug lets you one-hit bosses, and I don't think Bungie's gonna let this one slide
entry.content: Destiny 2's live service ambitions have come to an end with its final update last week. And with it, Bungie added everything and the kitchen sink, from new catalysts for every Exotic weapon to broken perk combinations. With no new endgame to grind or necessarily protect the sanctity of balance, Bungie's set us free with some of the most broken—and fun—tools we've ever had... Read more.

entry.title: I've been waiting 20 years for a worthy sequel to my favourite life sim, but THQ Nordic has other ideas
entry.content: I usually expect blank stares when I wax nostalgic about The Guild 2, a rough diamond that's part management game, part life sim, part RPG. It's the kind of game that does well in central Europe but not remotely as well elsewhere. Your classic eurojank romp: big on ambition, not so much on optimisation or clarity. This is the Stalker of life sims... Read more.

entry.title: MiG-21 Discounts for the Anniversary of the MiG-21’s First Flight!
entry.content: On June 15th 1955, the MiG-21, one of the most recognizable and mass-produced supersonic fighters in history, took to the skies for the first time. Nearly 11,500 of these aircraft were made. Relatively inexpensive to produce, it was also fast, survivable, and durable, ensuring its popularity worldwide and participation in numerous conflicts. The Chinese J-7 fighter family traces its lineage to the MiG-21. 30% Discount on MiG-21 variants! When: From June 16th (11:00 GMT) until June 19th (11:00 GM

entry.title: Destiny 2 comes full circle as players honour the tradition of pushing bosses to their deaths—this time by becoming a porcupine and smacking them with a void football
entry.content: Destiny 2 's sun is setting , and it's perhaps fitting that one of the oldest traditions of the franchise—that is, pushing bosses to their doom—is happening once again. Except this time, they're becoming porcupines holding void light footballs which smacks bosses so hard they fall to their deaths... Read more.

entry.title: Ironwood is hosting a Pacific Drive AMA!
entry.content: Join us this week on R/Gaming for a chance to ask all your burning questions about Pacific Drive! Our Summer 2026 AMA will be held on the R/Gaming subreddit this Thursday, June 18th 2026. entry.title: Both Final Fantasy 7 remakes are dirt cheap right now in a franchise sale—but I think you should play FF12 and enjoy the series' best combat system at a discount, too

entry.title: Dishonored dev defends loading screens: 'I actually have a soft spot for all that'
entry.content: Raphaël Colantonio, co-creative director of Dishonored, has been streaming a playthrough where he's joined by different members of the team at Arkane who worked on whichever level he's currently replaying. It's been interesting, and has resulted in the unearthing of titbits like Arkane being tapped to make Thief 4 and Blade Runner before they ended up with Dishonored... Read more.

entry.title: If you're bored of shop sims about restocking shelves, here's one about helping someone find the album that could change their life
entry.content: friend of mine who worked in a bookstore used to complain about customers who couldn't remember what they wanted. They'd say unhelpful things like, "I think it had a red cover?" At first the thought of gamifying that only with music instead of books seemed off-putting to me, as someone who goes shopping with overly detailed lists and would sooner bury myself in the ground than ask for help... Read more.

entry.title: Spore devs say the evolution game's previews were more ambitious than what they were actually making, and they 'built a fantasy in people's minds that was unachievable'
entry.content: Spore is the greatest game that never was. Clear as day, I remember the wonder the pitch instilled in me as a young'un. It was to be a true simulation like The Sims, not just of daily life, but of the history of all life— life as everything from a unicellular amoeba to a spacefaring alien of my own design, all realized in exacting detail... entry.title: Nintendo's legal battle with Palworld looks shakier than ever: It may only get '$30,000 chump change' if it wins at all
entry.content: Palworld's irreverent 'Pokémon with guns' conceit won it all sorts of buzz, but also attention from Nintendo's infamous lawyers—Palworld developer Pocketpair was sued by the big 'tendo back in 2024 over a few Pokémon patents for around $66,000 in damages—a small payout for such a big company, but with an apparent aim to bleed Pocketpair for millions more just fighting the suit... Read more.

entry.title: It's bittersweet that the best time to play Destiny 2 in years coincides with its end, but I'm happy for the new players who get to experience its most liberating iteration yet
entry.content: When Bungie announced the final update for Destiny 2 , I felt relieved. It surprised me as well. As someone who's spent the past six years (and over 3,000 hours) battling humanity's enemies across the solar system, you'd think that news of my favorite MMO's end would fill me with sadness. But no, just a bittersweet sense of relief as I considered we were finally moving on (as we should've after The Final Shape's year) to whatever the future may hold... Read more.

entry.title: This massively ambitious The Witcher 3 mod aims to recreate its biggest cut questline, and you could be the voice of Geralt of Rivia
entry.content: The Witcher 3 isn't exactly short of stuff to do, but I nonetheless lament that one of its most ambitious questlines never made it into the final game. Not only because it would have delved much more deeply into The Catriona Plague—a disease ravaging Velen that mostly serves as a background event in the final game—but also because it would have brought back The Witcher 2 's best character, the elven rebel smokeshow Iorveth... Read more.

entry.title: Palworld 1.0 won't wipe your data, 'but you should' says Pocketpair's publishing boss
entry.content: s announced at this year's Summer Games Fest, Palworld 1.0 arrives in less than a month. The launch version looks like it'll introduce a lot of new stuff to Pocketpair's Pokémon-inspired survival-crafting game, and with all those additions comes a question: will Palworld 1.0 require players to start again from scratch, or will you be able to carry on your adventure as normal?.. Read more.

entry.title: Even a fantasy game like Skyrim won't let me be a trillionaire
entry.content: The news that Elon Musk has become the world's first trillionaire, which seems like something the world definitely doesn't need, got me thinking: have I ever even played a videogame that let me be a trillionaire? I've definitely racked up millions of dollars or coins or other make-believe currency in a few games… but trillions?.. Read more.

entry.title: Weekend XP bonus is active!
entry.content: The global XP bonus is back - it's time to set new records on Icebreaker! This weekend, XP earned from raids will be increased by 25% - both in PvP and PvE. This is a fixed value for all players and is applied instead of the edition bonus. The bonus will be available until June 15, 11:00 AM BST / 6:00 AM EST.

entry.title: Over the Hill's Next Fest demo promises a stylized off-roading game for people who actually like to drive
entry.content: I was never able to get a feel for Funselektor's top-down driving game Art of Rally , or its predecessor Absolute Drift: I like rally in real life, but sliding cars around corners at screaming speeds from a weird perspective and with no tactile feedback just didn't work for me. I had higher hopes for the slower pace of the studio's upcoming Over the Hill , and after trying the demo that dropped today with the start of the Steam Next Fest, I am very happy to say that yeah, this is the one... entry.title: A Decal Trophy and Discounts on Rare German Ships for German Navy Day!

entry.title: Why do mech games rarely let you leave the cockpit? Brigador Killers devs joke that the feature 'added five years of development time'
entry.content: I love a mech game, but I especially love when a mech game lets you get out of the cockpit and just run around. It makes things feel so much less gamey , and it more fully sells the scale and power of a mech to contrast it with the perspective of a puny little human. Few games ever do it, though... Read more.

entry.title: Time to get a little sadder about Destiny 2: TWIDs are coming to an end
entry.content: Destiny 2 is ending—for all intents and purposes, in fact, it already has, with the launch earlier this week of its final update, Monument of Triumph . More than one Destiny fan at PC Gamer has described the moment as bittersweet—it's not only Destiny 2's last live service update, it's also one of its best —and in a smaller way, this is too: Maybe it's no surprise, but the clockwork-like This Week in Destiny updates will soon be over... Read more.

entry.title: Odświeżona strona główna Steam
entry.content: Dzisiaj z przyjemnością ogłaszamy wydanie odświeżonej strony głównej Sklepu Steam. entry.title: Steamworks SDK w wersji 1.64 już dostępne
entry.content: Steamworks SDK v1.64 zostało wydane i jest dostępne do pobrania na stronie https://partner.steamgames.com/ . Podobnie jak w przypadku innych wydań Steamworks SDK, aktualizacja jest zalecana, ale nie wymagana – starsze wersje będą działać bez zarzutu. Lista zmian Ogólne Od teraz biblioteki androidarm64 łączą statycznie bibliotekę libc++. entry.title: Community Update #36 A Very Moddy Christmas
entry.content: Hello friends, The days are getting colder, the holiday decorations are creeping out early, and honestly? We’re leaning into it. It’s been a long year, one that saw Baldur’s Gate 3 receive twelve new subclasses, full cross-play support, and the arrival of photo mode - the last final, final last patch.
